Okay, welcome back. So now that you've done your bio and solidified those beliefs, it's time to really add some fastener, some sealant, or whatever appropriate home renovation term works for you just to make sure those beliefs really stick. And to do that, you're going to use an upgraded form of the good old testimonial, which is actually one of the most misunderstood parts of marketing. So what you need to understand right off the bat is that the main role of the testimonial is not to show how great you or your product are. And it's not even to ramp up desire by pointing at the end result that comes with using your product. So what is the testimonial? The testimonial is a mini story in which your prospect gets to experience the complete transformation through a proxy and in doing so prepare themselves to make that journey themselves. So it's not so much about trusting in your ability to generate results, but in learning to trust themselves to get the results with your help. And the best way to do that is for them to relate with others who started that journey with the same moment of highest tension and across that bridge successfully. So more specifically, here's what this client proxy testimonial is teaching. So the first thing you're coaching, just like the last section is trust, but this time in their ability to generate results with your help. And we're going to do that through identifying with past client success stories, using other client stories as a proxy for the journey they're about to embark on. And we have to do that through creating a common starting point. They need to see that moment of highest tension, that bleeding neck pain point is the same for them to really go on that moment of highest tension journey and relatability. And we're going to also do that with an exciting outer edge of possibility results. So this is typically what most testimonials start and end with is just the result. And sometimes the result is too big and crazy for someone to even relate with. So if you really want your past client successes to be a good proxy for current prospects, that result has to be on that outer edge of possibility. So that's what we're going to be coaching here. The basic structure for doing it is going to be as follows. So we're going to start off with those bleeding neck pain points. So the moment of highest tension that your client was experiencing before they hired you or your product. Now this is single-handedly the most important part. Nobody cares that you lost 20 pounds on a keto diet unless they know you were addicted to Doritos and wasted $75 a month on an unused gold gym membership for the past two years before that. So the testimonial is a proxy for your reader and the only way they can experience that outcome and success as their own is if they first recognize themselves in that pre. The next thing the testimonials is going to have is that tipping point or precursor to hiring you or your product. So this is all about something geeky called mirror neurons. So if your prospect can see the exact same precursor that triggered this action that resulted in the positive outcome, it creates that connection. It makes them realize that X condition must be met with Y response, which can then generate Z outcome. And then the final part of the testimonial is really the results of the decision to work with you. So even here, your customer under prospect using your customer as a proxy is still the hero of the story. So it's not you. It was their decision, which was the heroic move. Now all this said, so this is the basic structure. And when asking clients for testimonials, I do recommend you make it easier on them by just prompting them with a series of questions. So here's some questions that I like to send a client when getting testimonials. So the first question would be, what specifically were you experiencing in regards to the problem before hiring me? And this is going to give you a lot of that moment of highest tension detail, as well as the precursors, which really comes into play in number two, which is what was the specific tipping point that made you realize you needed help. So what is that common tipping point? And finally, what have been the specific results of our work together? What have been some unexpected benefits or wins? And what aspect of our work together made the biggest impact for you? So when you ask these three questions in this order, you get the perfect structure of a testimonial. So before we wrap up, I want to give you one example from 10xLogis. This was from my friend Kelsey Kaufman at Green Tea Productions. And she wrote, I always struggled to make my launch emails do all the things they needed to do. I wanted to get the word out and encourage people to purchase our courses, but I also didn't want to sound or feel like a sleazy marketer or drive people crazy. So there you have the moment of highest tension detail. After one phone call with Ryan, I knew I never wanted to do another launch with Adam. I think he might be a mind reader. Not only did he make the process streamline and fun for me, but his immense well of empathy makes him the absolute best collaborator. On top of that, he helped me to find my voice. Now I'm proud of the emails we send out leading up to a launch because students love them. I actually get thank you notes from my list when I send out a note I've worked on with Ryan. That's exactly what I needed to hear. Or this is exactly what I'm going through. Thanks so much. So those last two paragraphs really give that outer edge of possibility result. You know, a very exciting outcome that's achievable for anyone really reading this. Then just some self-lattery at the end. Ryan is the best. I'm way more confident about my copy and serve many more students as a direct result of working with him. One example I encourage you to take the prompts. I just gave you in the last slide and collect your own and really try to follow that template. Now one last advanced application is segmentation. If you are segmenting your list and have different categories of customers. For example, in copy hackers we have copywriters and we have business owners, entrepreneurs, growth marketers, startup founders. One of the easiest applications of segmentation is changing up the testimonials. So collect testimonials that best reflect different avatars or different personas that you market to. So that's one extra little advanced application that if you are doing some advanced segmentation, you'll want to apply. And with all that said, it's time to put this to work to write or collect your client proxy testimonials before meeting me in the next writing workshop. I'll see you there.
